Prompt
U
Oracle Events
- Search Linear for the duplicate-cluster / follow-through lane using linear_list_issues (query: "ENG-220" or "duplicate detection" or "TechComfort" or "manual review threshold" or similar). The agent needs to find ENG-220 as the main duplicate-detection ticket, plus the adjacent engineering remediation context that matters to this prompt: ENG-217 (pipeline hardening, still in progress), ENG-218 (dashboard validation, still in progress), and ENG-219 (launch readiness checklist, still to do).
- Read ENG-220 and its related ticket detail using linear_get_issue and/or linear_list_comments. The agent needs to confirm that ENG-220 is still in To Do, urgent, assigned to Dmitri Volkov, and still carries the full bundled acceptance criteria: exact hash matching, PDF metadata similarity, cross-client scope, dashboard duplicate-status visibility, the $100 manual-review rule, backfill against existing Airtable receipts, and unit-test coverage. The agent should also confirm that Dmitri filed it after the audit and that Samira had already prioritized it.
- Read adjacent engineering ticket context using linear_get_issue and/or linear_list_comments on ENG-217 and optionally ENG-219. The agent needs to confirm that the broader remediation stack is real - ENG-217 is in progress and carries the audit results / hardening work, while ENG-219 is still to do - but that this does not mean the duplicate-control work in ENG-220 has actually started.
- Search Linear for whether the remaining engineering control gap is already separately tracked using linear_list_issues (query: "$100" or "manual review" or "manual verification" or "threshold" or "backfill" or similar). The agent needs to confirm there is no separate issue that cleanly owns the $100 manual-review control gap yet, which is why one follow-up issue still needs to be created.
- Search Airtable for the three flagged stipend records using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: "Stipend Transactions", field_name: "Employee Name", value: "Kevin Tran" / "Andre Washington" / "Jordan Ekwueme"). The agent needs to find rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard, rec_stipend_andre_keyboard, and rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand in tblStipends00001, with the duplicate-hash and template-match evidence in Review Notes: Kevin and Andre share the same TechComfort keyboard receipt hash, while Jordan's monitor-stand receipt is a different file but from the same TC-RECEIPT-STD-041 template cluster.
- Search Airtable for Kevin Tran's broader stipend history using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: "Stipend Transactions", field_name: "Employee Name", value: "Kevin Tran"). The agent needs to find the approved $895 ergonomic chair, approved $189 webcam / microphone, and approved $185 internet item(s) as evidence that Elena Rostova's over-$100 manual-verification rule is not cleanly reflected in the current live record state.
- Search QuickBooks for the stipend transactions and hold records using quickbooks_search_bills (fetchAll: true, or a query around "STIP-2026" / "TechComfort" if needed). The agent needs to find STIP-2026-0089, STIP-2026-0094, and STIP-2026-0097, confirm that Kevin Tran's $89 keyboard was already paid on March 8, 2026, and confirm that Andre Washington's $89 keyboard and Jordan Ekwueme's $45 monitor stand are still unpaid and on admin hold. This is the source that makes the hold state real even though Airtable still shows the held records as Pending Approval.
- Search Slack for the duplicate-cluster discussion and the policy directive using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "TechComfort" or "ENG-220" or "duplicate receipt" or "manual verification" or "STIP-2026" or similar). The agent needs to find Dmitri Volkov's duplicate-cluster post, the ENG-220 filing discussion, and Elena Rostova's directive that all stipend reimbursements over $100 require manual verification before payment and must be flagged in Airtable.
- Search email for the audit / verification follow-through using search_emails (query: "TechComfort" or "STIP-2026-0089" or "Kevin Tran" or "duplicate anomaly" or similar). The agent needs to find Marcus Thorne's verification ask on Kevin Tran, the duplicate-anomaly thread laying out the three-record cluster and the $223 exposure, later Vectral-side evidence that Kevin Tran's flagged receipt was treated as legitimate, and the lack of any clear email resolution that closes the HR-verification loop for Andre Washington and Jordan Ekwueme.
- Add a reality-check comment to ENG-220 using linear_create_comment. The comment should document the verified cross-system picture: Kevin's reimbursement already paid, Andre's and Jordan's reimbursements still held, the cluster evidence confirmed across Airtable / QuickBooks / Slack / email, and the fact that ENG-220 follow-through is still largely unstarted despite the existing ticket and discussion.
- Create one follow-up Linear issue using linear_create_issue. The issue should narrowly track the still-unowned engineering control gap around the $100 manual-review threshold, with related logging or backfill language only if it stays tightly tied to that control. It should not balloon into a full rewrite of the entire DuplicateDetector pipeline spec.
- Look up Samira Tariq's contact using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Samira" or "Tariq" or similar). The agent needs to find samira.tariq@moveops.com.
- Send Samira a short internal note using send_email (to: samira.tariq@moveops.com). The email should separate what is confirmed real now from what is still open or unimplemented. Samira is the only explicit stakeholder recipient required by this prompt.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-1-moveops-v3

Rubric Results
✓ Pass ✗ Fail Click any row to expand justifications
# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 The `linear_create_comment` body states that Kevin Tran's `$89` keyboard / `STIP-2026-0089` was already paid.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The linear_create_comment body includes a table row for Kevin Tran showing QB balance $0 (paid) and Airtable Approved, with 'Confirmed paid' in the Match column.
✓Run #2The linear_create_comment body explicitly states 'Kevin Tran | rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard | Approved | STIP-2026-0089 | $0.00 | No — paid 3/8' with a '✅ Yes' alignment indicator, clearly stating the $89 keyboard was already paid.
✓Run #3The linear_create_comment body on ENG-220 explicitly states: 'Kevin Tran | Vectral Systems | ErgoKey Pro keyboard | $89 | Approved | **Paid** (Balance $0, STIP-2026-0089, paid 3/8)'
✓Run #4The `linear_create_comment` body explicitly states: 'Kevin Tran (Vectral) | rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard / STIP-2026-0089 | Approved, $89 | Balance $0 — Paid 3/8 | ✅ Matches ticket'
✓Run #5The linear_create_comment body explicitly states: 'STIP-2026-0089 (Kevin Tran, Vectral, $89 keyboard): Paid 3/8. Balance $0. This is done and cannot be reversed.'
✓Run #6The linear_create_comment body explicitly states: 'Kevin Tran (Vectral) | STIP-2026-0089 | Approved | Balance $0 | **Yes — paid 3/8**' and 'Financial exposure confirmed: $89 disbursed.' PASS.

outcome

✓Run #6The linear_create_comment body explicitly states: 'Andre Washington (Mosaic) | STIP-2026-0094 | Pending Approval | ADMIN HOLD (Marcus, 3/15) | **No — on hold**'. PASS.

outcome

outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The linear_create_comment body explicitly shows a cross-system match table where all three records show ✅ alignment between Airtable and QuickBooks. The 'Financial exposure confirmed' line also summarizes the cross-system picture.
✓Run #2The linear_create_comment body includes a full Airtable ↔ QuickBooks alignment table with an 'Aligned?' column showing ✅ Yes for all three records, and explicitly states 'All three records are consistent between Airtable and QuickBooks.'
✓Run #3The linear_create_comment body includes: 'The paid/hold status described in the ticket matches both QuickBooks and Airtable... No drift between systems on the financial side.' This is explicit cross-system consistency language.
✓Run #4The `linear_create_comment` body states: 'Holds are real and in place in both Airtable and QuickBooks' and each record row is marked '✅ Matches ticket', confirming cross-system alignment.
✓Run #5The linear_create_comment body explicitly states: 'All three Airtable records are consistent with the QuickBooks state. Holds and flags are correctly reflected in both systems.'
✓Run #6The linear_create_comment body includes a table showing both Airtable Status and QuickBooks Status columns and explicitly states 'This matches the original finding — no additional payments slipped through,' confirming cross-system alignment. PASS.
5 The created issue's title or description centers on the `$100` manual-review / manual-verification threshold or equivalent `DecisionEngine` control-gap wording.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The created issue title is 'Implement $100 manual-review threshold & backfill receipt fingerprints (ENG-220 untracked gap)' and the description extensively discusses the $100 threshold and DecisionEngine.
✓Run #2The linear_create_issue title is '$100 manual review threshold — enforce in DecisionEngine and verify Airtable logging', which clearly centers on the $100 manual-review threshold and DecisionEngine control gap.
✓Run #3The created issue title is: 'ENG-220 follow-up: $100 manual review threshold + receipt backfill scan — untracked gaps' — directly centered on the $100 manual review threshold control gap.
✓Run #4The `linear_create_issue` title is '$100 manual-review threshold rule — implement in DecisionEngine (CEO directive, still missing)', which directly centers on the $100 threshold / DecisionEngine control gap.
✓Run #5The linear_create_issue title is 'Implement $100 manual verification threshold in DecisionEngine (Elena directive 3/15)' and the description centers on the $100 manual verification threshold as a control gap.
✓Run #6The linear_create_issue title is 'Implement Layer 1 hash dedup + $100 manual review threshold — unblock ENG-220.' The description includes a dedicated section '### 2. $100 manual review threshold (Elena's directive, 3/15)' and references 'DecisionEngine'. PASS.
6 The created issue body states that the `$100` manual-review threshold should be tracked independently from the broader duplicate-detection / `DuplicateDetector` work, or equivalent standalone-control wording.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The created issue body explicitly states 'These two items are: Independent of the pipeline redesign' and includes a dedicated section 'Why this is separate from ENG-220', stating both items can ship independently of the full DuplicateDetector.
✓Run #2The linear_create_issue description explicitly states 'This was scoped in ENG-220 but that ticket is 32 days past due with no work started. This control needs independent tracking because it's a CEO-mandated policy, not just a nice-to-have' — clearly framing the $100 threshold as separate from the broader duplicate detection work.
✓Run #3The created issue body explicitly states: 'Even if the DuplicateDetector pipeline work starts soon, the $100 threshold and backfill are independent workstreams that should be tracked separately so they don't get lost again.' This is clear standalone-control wording.
✓Run #4The `linear_create_issue` body explicitly states: 'The $100 threshold is a standalone policy control in the DecisionEngine — it does not depend on the DuplicateDetector pipeline stage (Layer 1/Layer 2). It can and should be shipped independently.'
✓Run #5The linear_create_issue description explicitly states: 'The $100 threshold is an independent policy control that can and should be shipped separately' and 'ENG-220 duplicate detection work is blocked/stalled, but this rule is independent and can ship in isolation.'
✓Run #6The created issue's '## Out of scope (stays in ENG-220)' section explicitly separates Layer 2 PDF metadata scoring, dashboard, and cross-client comparison from this issue — tracking the $100 threshold and Layer 1 as a focused scope independent from the broader DuplicateDetector work. PASS.
7 The created issue stays engineering-scoped and does not make AM, finance, or client-HR follow-up the main issue scope.
outcome
 ✓—✓✓✓✓ 83%
✓Run #1The issue is engineering-scoped, focusing on DecisionEngine threshold implementation and backfill script. The acceptance criteria are technical engineering tasks. No AM/finance/client-HR follow-up is the main scope.
—Run #2The issue title and primary focus is engineering-scoped (DecisionEngine, Airtable logging). However, the description includes a 'Process (AM + Finance)' section referencing Marcus/Hana, which somewhat broadens the scope beyond pure engineering. The main scope remains engineering, so this partially passes but the AM/finance section is notable.
✓Run #3The created issue's main scope is engineering-focused: implementing the $100_THRESHOLD_MANUAL_REVIEW rule in DecisionEngine, running a backfill script, and shipping the dashboard duplicate status column. AM/finance/client-HR personnel are not the main issue scope.
✓Run #4The `linear_create_issue` is scoped to implementing the $100 threshold rule in the DecisionEngine, with engineering acceptance criteria (rule implementation, unit tests, backfill). No AM/finance/client-HR person is the main subject.
